## Overseas names

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Isle_of_Wight&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: Overseas names")]

The Isle of Wight has given names to many parts of former colonies, most notably [Isle of Wight County](/wiki/Isle_of_Wight_County "Isle of Wight County") in [Virginia](/wiki/Virginia "Virginia") founded by settlers from the island in the 17th century. Its [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat") is a town named Isle of Wight.

Other notable examples include:

* [Isle of Wight](/wiki/Isle_of_Wight_(Maryland) "Isle of Wight (Maryland)") – an island off Maryland, United States
* [Dunnose Head, West Falkland](/wiki/Dunnose_Head,_West_Falkland "Dunnose Head, West Falkland")
* Ventnor, Cowes on [Phillip Island](/wiki/Phillip_Island "Phillip Island"), Victoria, Australia
* [Carisbrook, Victoria](/wiki/Carisbrook,_Victoria "Carisbrook, Victoria"), Australia
* [Carisbrook](/wiki/Carisbrook "Carisbrook"), a former stadium in Dunedin, New Zealand
* [Ryde, New South Wales](/wiki/Ryde,_New_South_Wales "Ryde, New South Wales"), Australia
* Shanklin, [Sandown, New Hampshire](/wiki/Sandown,_New_Hampshire "Sandown, New Hampshire"), United States
* [Ventnor City](/wiki/Ventnor_City,_New_Jersey "Ventnor City, New Jersey"), New Jersey, United States
* [Gardiners Island](/wiki/Gardiners_Island "Gardiners Island"), New York, United States shown as "Isle of Wight" on some of the older maps.[[157]](#cite_note-161)

### Games

* John Worsley's [Commodore 64](/wiki/Commodore_64 "Commodore 64") game *[Spirit of the Stones](/wiki/Spirit_of_the_Stones "Spirit of the Stones")* was set on the Isle of Wight.[[158]](#cite_note-162)

### Music

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Isle_of_Wight&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: Music")]

* [The Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles")' song "*[When I'm Sixty-Four](/wiki/When_I%27m_Sixty-Four "When I'm Sixty-Four")*" (1967), credited to [Lennon-McCartney](/wiki/Lennon-McCartney "Lennon-McCartney") and sung by [Paul McCartney](/wiki/Paul_McCartney "Paul McCartney"), refers to renting a cottage on the island.[[159]](#cite_note-163)
* The Italian pop-band [Dik Dik](/wiki/Dik_Dik "Dik Dik")'s single [*L'Isola di Wight*](/wiki/Dik_Dik#Singles "Dik Dik") (1970), which uses the island as a metaphor for youthful freedom and travel.

### Television

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Isle_of_Wight&action=edit&section=42 "Edit section: Television")]

* *[Survivors](/wiki/Survivors_(1975_TV_series) "Survivors (1975 TV series)")*, the BBC's 1970s post-apocalyptic sci-fi drama set after a worldwide pandemic kills off most of humanity, features an episode in which 500 survivors holed up in London are to be relocated to the Isle of Wight. Though referred to many times in the Series 2 episode "Lights of London – Part 2", the move itself is not shown (nor any footage of the island).[[160]](#cite_note-164)
* ITV's dramatisation of [Dennis Potter](/wiki/Dennis_Potter "Dennis Potter")'s work *[Blade on the Feather](/wiki/Blade_on_the_Feather "Blade on the Feather")* (19 October 1980) was filmed on the island.[[161]](#cite_note-165)
* A 2002 *[Top Gear](/wiki/Top_Gear_(2002_TV_series) "Top Gear (2002 TV series)")* feature showed an [Aston Martin](/wiki/Aston_Martin "Aston Martin") being driven around Cowes, East Cowes, and along the Military Road and seawall at Freshwater Bay.[[162]](#cite_note-166)
* The setting for *[Free Rein](/wiki/Free_Rein "Free Rein")* was based on the Isle of Wight.[[163]](#cite_note-167)
* Portions of the 2021 drama series *[It's a Sin](/wiki/It%27s_a_Sin_(TV_series) "It's a Sin (TV series)")* on [Channel 4](/wiki/Channel_4 "Channel 4") were supposedly set in the Isle of Wight, the home of one of the lead characters, although they were actually filmed in [Rhos-on-Sea](/wiki/Rhos-on-Sea "Rhos-on-Sea")[[164]](#cite_note-168) and [Bangor](/wiki/Bangor,_Gwynedd "Bangor, Gwynedd")[[165]](#cite_note-169) in north Wales.
* The sitcom *[The Cockfields](/wiki/The_Cockfields "The Cockfields")* is set on the Isle of Wight.[[166]](#cite_note-170)

### Novels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Isle_of_Wight&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Novels")]

[Julian Barnes](/wiki/Julian_Barnes "Julian Barnes")' novel *[England, England](/wiki/England,_England "England, England")* broaches the idea of replicating England in a theme park on the Isle of Wight.[[167]](#cite_note-171)

Retrieved 4 February 2024. "The British Deer Society understands that the Isle of Wight Biodiversity Group wishes to designate the Island as a 'deer-free zone'. Red and Roe deer can be considered indigenous to the Island and, despite periods of absence, both species are continually recorded as swimming to the Island from the mainland. Their presence may therefore be considered to be natural and part of a rich biodiversity. Our ancient woodlands were shaped in the presence of deer, and browsing by deer is an important and beneficial factor in creating diverse woodland structure. As wild deer, these species have a clear entitlement to remain within their natural range and it is manifestly inappropriate to write them out of the Island's natural history. If management is required, then both species are readily managed with human intervention."
